title: Subagent Nesting Guard and Config Slots
description: Expose general-purpose in pi-crew config and prevent invoked sub-agents from launching nested sub-agents by default.

work_packages.title: Config slot coverage
work_packages.description: Make general-purpose visible and configurable in the existing pi-crew configuration flow while preserving inheritance behavior.
verification_commands: npx vitest run test/unit/config-auto.test.ts test/unit/config-schema.test.ts test/unit/slot.test.ts test/unit/tool-schema.test.ts
work_packages.rationale: The slot list, default suggestion behavior, and inheritance tests form one small configuration surface that should be changed and verified together.

work_packages.title: Nested subagent runtime guard
work_packages.description: Enforce code-level nested sub-agent suppression in both session and subprocess execution paths, including prompt/tool exposure tests.
verification_commands: npx vitest run test/unit/session-lifecycle.test.ts test/unit/spawn.test.ts test/unit/system-prompt.test.ts
work_packages.rationale: Session-mode and subprocess-mode suppression share the same safety goal and tool-name contract, so one implementer should keep the guard consistent across runtime paths.

phases.name: Configuration slot visibility
phases.description: General-purpose becomes configurable through the existing slash command without losing parent-model inheritance when unset.

tasks.title: Expose general-purpose in config flow
tasks.description: Update the config slot list so `/subagent-config` presents both `explore` and `general-purpose`. Keep the existing skip/unset behavior so general-purpose can remain inherited when the user does not explicitly configure it.
acceptance_criteria: The exported config slot list contains both `explore` and `general-purpose`.
Default suggestion behavior does not force-create a `general-purpose` slot when the user has not configured one.
The config TUI still saves selected provider, model, and thinking values for every listed slot.
Dispatch uses a saved `general-purpose` slot when one exists and falls back to inheritance only when it is unset.
tasks.context: Covers REQ-1, REQ-2, REQ-3, AC-1, AC-2, and AC-3. The user noticed that only explore appears in `/subagent-config`, but general-purpose should also be configurable and meaningful when saved.

tasks.title: Verify inheritance remains intact
tasks.description: Add or update focused tests proving that an explicit general-purpose slot is honored and that parent model/thinking inheritance applies only when no slot exists. Keep tests aligned with existing config and slot-resolution patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Tests fail if `general-purpose` is removed from the config slot list.
Tests continue to prove `suggestDefaults()` leaves `general-purpose` unset by default.
Tests prove `resolveAgentSlot()` honors an explicit configured `general-purpose` slot.
Tests prove `resolveAgentSlot()` inherits parent model/thinking when `general-purpose` is unset.
tasks.context: Covers REQ-2, REQ-3, AC-2, and AC-3. The config UI change must not accidentally turn general-purpose into a mandatory slot or a no-op configured slot.

phases.name: Nested subagent prevention
phases.description: Sub-agents lose access to pi-crew delegation tools and do not receive prompt guidance encouraging nested delegation.

tasks.title: Define pi-crew tool suppression contract
tasks.description: Create a shared code-level contract for pi-crew orchestration tools that must be hidden from invoked sub-agents. Include `Agent`, all `subagent_*` orchestration tools, `get_subagent_result`, and `steer_subagent`.
acceptance_criteria: The suppression list includes `Agent`, `subagent_dispatch`, `subagent_run`, `subagent_status`, `get_subagent_result`, `steer_subagent`, and `subagent_kill`.
Runtime code imports or reuses the shared contract instead of duplicating a partial local list.
A focused test fails if one of the required pi-crew orchestration tool names is omitted from the contract.
tasks.context: Covers REQ-4, REQ-5, and AC-4. The previous session-mode filter missed `Agent`, which allowed a general-purpose sub-agent to launch another sub-agent.

tasks.title: Enforce guard in session-mode subagents
tasks.description: Apply the shared suppression contract when creating session-mode sub-agent sessions. The guard must remove pi-crew orchestration tools even when custom agent frontmatter omits `tools:` or includes those names.
acceptance_criteria: Session-mode sub-agents do not have active pi-crew orchestration tools after session creation.
Denied pi-crew orchestration tools remain inactive after extension binding or tool refresh.
Filtering still preserves ordinary allowed tools such as `read` and `bash` and preserves non-pi-crew extension behavior where practical.
Tests cover `Agent`, `get_subagent_result`, and `steer_subagent` in addition to the older `subagent_*` names.
tasks.context: Covers REQ-4, REQ-5, REQ-6, AC-4, and AC-8. Users should not need to know internal tool names in agent markdown.

tasks.title: Enforce guard in subprocess-mode subagents
tasks.description: Ensure subprocess-mode children launched by pi-crew suppress pi-crew orchestration tool registration. Parent sessions must keep registering tools normally, while child processes launched as sub-agents must not.
acceptance_criteria: Subprocess launch includes an explicit pi-crew-owned child marker, `PI_CREW_SUPPRESS_SUBAGENT_TOOLS=1`, for invoked sub-agents.
When `PI_CREW_SUPPRESS_SUBAGENT_TOOLS=1` is present, pi-crew does not register orchestration tools in that process.
When the marker is absent, parent sessions still register the existing pi-crew tools.
Tests distinguish the new pi-crew marker from existing lineage variables such as `PI_SUBAGENT_PARENT_ID` and `PI_SUBAGENT_SESSION_ID`.
tasks.context: Covers REQ-6, AC-5, and AC-7. The code-level guard must work for both execution backends without disabling legitimate parent sessions.

tasks.title: Suppress subagent prompt guidance for children
tasks.description: Prevent invoked sub-agents from receiving the pi-crew system-prompt block that advertises delegation tools. Keep the prompt block available to normal parent sessions where pi-crew tools are active.
acceptance_criteria: Session-mode final child system prompt content does not include the `## pi-crew sub-agents` guidance block.

Subprocess-mode tests exercise child pi-crew extension startup under `PI_CREW_SUPPRESS_SUBAGENT_TOOLS=1` and assert the final system prompt delivered after `before_agent_start` omits the `## pi-crew sub-agents` block.
Subprocess-mode persisted child prompt content also does not include the `## pi-crew sub-agents` guidance block.
Unmarked parent session prompts still include pi-crew guidance when pi-crew is loaded normally.
tasks.context: Covers REQ-7, REQ-8, AC-6, and AC-7. Removing tools alone is insufficient if the child prompt still tells sub-agents to delegate.
